text:
DisappointedOlympics 2024viewers were all left making the same complaint as theParis opening ceremony failed to impress.

As the French faced bad luck with the weather, and fan backlash over a drawn-out format of the countries being introduced by boat, UK viewers took to social media in their droves to praise Danny Boyle.

Boyle had been the mastermind behind the epic London ceremony, which saw thousands of volunteers, including the Queen, put on a huge performance for thrilled watchers.

Other legendary British stars took part in the glitzy ceremony, including the Spice Girls, who reunited on stage for a jaw-dropping performance, as well as George Michael and Paul McCartney.

Fans rushed to social media to make the brutal comparison, as one said: "Danny Boyle and Graham Norton trending, just goes to show that no one puts on a show like The Irish."





We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Another added: "Danny Boyle must be feeling very smug. This OC has looked patchy, disjointed and not very entertaining. 2012 was wonderful and memorable."

A third chimed in: "This STOP THE BOATS #OpeningCeremony by the French in Paris falls far far short of Danny Boyle's. Happy and Glorious. London, 2012. Bond ... James Bond."

A fourth added: "This is a very long reminder of the brilliance of Danny Boyle."

A fifth agreed: "12 years later and ten minutes in and this #OpeningCeremony is clearly coming nowhere near to London 2012," as a sixth grumbled: "Sorry but this has been pretty tedious saved by the beauty of Paris and some brilliant dancing - it’s certainly no #London2012."



Meanwhile, iconic singerCeline Dion left viewers worried for heras she still hadn't turned up for the ceremony after hours of waiting, sparking fears she wouldn't perform.

Celine had been pictured in Paris, and had shared her love for the city with anInstagrampost, penning: "Every time I return to Paris, I remember there’s so much beauty and joy still to experience in the world.

"I love Paris, and I’m so happy to be back! Thank you to our wonderful friends at The Louvre!"

However, due to her Stiff Person Syndrome causing her constant pain and health issues, fans feared she was going to pull out at the last second.

title: Paris Olympics opening ceremony viewers all say same thing in brutal 2012 comparison | TV & Radio | Showbiz & TV | Express.co.uk
author: Sasha Morris
description: BBC Olympics 2024 viewers were all left saying the same thing as they reminisced about London 2012's epic opening ceremony.
